'''Ryan Boyle''' (born 17 October 1987) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} forward in the [[Super League]], the [[RFL Championship]] and [[RFL League 1|League 1]] in the 2000s, 2010s and 2020s. He represented [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at international level.

Boyle made over 100 appearances for his hometown club the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] across two separate spells. He spent three and a half seasons with the [[Salford City Reds]] in Super League. He played in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], initially on loan from Castleford before signing permanently, then moved to [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] where he spent four seasons.

==Background==
Boyle was born in [[Leeds]], [[Yorkshire]], England.

He progressed through the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy system.

==Playing career==
Boyle made his début for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in 2005.<ref name="BBC4apr13">{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22029175|title= Salford & Castleford swap Ryan Boyle & Jacob Emmitt|publisher=BBC Sport|date=4 April 2013|accessdate=21 January 2018}}</ref>

In October 2009, Boyle was selected for the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Irish national squad]] for the [[2009 European Cup|European Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rte.ie/sport/rugby/2009/1008/255783-rugbyleague|title=Irish Rugby League squad announced|publisher=RTE|date=8 October 2009|accessdate=21 January 2018}}</ref>

Boyle moved to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in 2010<ref name="BBC4apr13"/> but in April 2013 returned to Castleford<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 April 2013 |title=Salford & Castleford swap Ryan Boyle & Jacob Emmitt |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22029175 |access-date=10 January 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and in October 2013 he signed a two-year extension to his contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24652841|title=Castleford Tigers: Ryan Boyle signs contract extension|publisher=BBC Sport|date=24 October 2013|accessdate=21 January 2018}}</ref>

At the end of April 2016, it was reported that Boyle had signed for [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] on [[Rugby League Dual registration|dual registration]],<ref>{{cite web|url =https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls/championship-sheffield-deny-dewsbury-bradford-turn-to-academy-talent-ryan-boyle-joins-batley-1-7885859|title=Championship: Sheffield deny Dewsbury, Bradford turn to Academy talent, Ryan Boyle joins Batley|publisher=The Yorkshire Post|date=30 April 2016|accessdate=21 January 2018}}</ref> but three weeks later he joined [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] on a loan deal until the end of the season,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxrlfc.co.uk/article/48724/ryan-boyle-joins-on-loan-until-end-of-the-season|title=Ryan Boyle joins on loan until end of the season|publisher=Halifax R.L.F.C.|date=20 May 2016|accessdate=21 January 2018}}</ref> in which he made 16 appearances and scored one try. The move was made permanent in October 2016 with a deal for the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/halifax-complete-signing-of-prop-boyle-from-castleford-tigers-1-8189864|title=Halifax complete signing of prop Boyle from Castleford Tigers|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=19 October 2016|accessdate=21 January 2018}}</ref>

In October 2017, Boyle joined [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] from [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/doncaster-snap-seven-players-2018/ |title=Doncaster snap up seven players for 2018|publisher=Love Rugby League |date=21 October 2017|accessdate=17 January 2018}}</ref>

Boyle announced his retirement from rugby league following the conclusion of the 2021 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 January 2022 |title=I've got no regrets - Boyle |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p0bfppl7 |access-date=10 January 2024 |website=BBC Radio Leeds}}</ref> He began working as a plumbing and heating engineer.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Contacts the key for players as Ryan plots future success |url=https://www.rugbyleaguecares.org/news/players/contacts-the-key-for-players-as-ryan-plots-future-success/ |access-date=10 January 2024 |website=Rugby League Cares|date=19 May 2021 }}</ref>
